calendar.matters: REPORT OF STATUS OF ADMINISTRATION
PETITION FOR INSTRUCTIONS

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Hearing For Filing Of A Petition For Final Distribution Or A Petition For Approval Of Report On Status Of Administration
Off Calendar Apr-13-2026 Off Calendar

REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (19 Blair Terrace, San Francisco, Ca 94107)
Off Calendar Dec-17-2025 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Rowena Navia for petitioner Kevin Nguy, who was also present (via Zoom).
Jonathan Do for Huong Pham, decedent's surviving spouse (via Zoom).
Ryan Grimm, buyer.
Jennifer Nguyen, decedent's daughter (via Zoom).
Kelly Quon and Sabrina G. Shin, listing agents.

The matter was called for hearing on Petition for Order Confirming Sale of Real Property.

Mr. Do informed the Court that his client was currently in the hospital and unable to personally attend the hearing. However, he stated she had expressed interest in purchasing the property and was prepared to overbid in open court on her behalf.

The Court conducted an overbid auction, with the starting overbid in the amount of $630,500. After multiple overbids, the highest and final overbid was made by Huong Pham, through their counsel Mr. Do, in the amount of $710,500.

The Court confirmed the sale of the real property to Huong Pham for $710,500. Ms. Navia indicated she would coordinate receipt of the required certified funds and handle the remaining steps to complete the sale.

Judge: Ross C. Moody; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

REPORT OF STATUS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Jul-21-2025 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Rowena Navia for and with Kevin Nguy, Administrator (via Zoom).
Jonathan Do for Huong Pham, Surviving Spouse (via Zoom).

Discussion was held regarding the Objection filed by Huong Pham. Mr. Do confirmed that the objection was limited to raising concerns about the status of Jennifer Nguyen as an heir, noting that no proof of her relationship to the decedent had been provided. He did not oppose extending administration but emphasized the importance of resolving this and other outstanding issues.

Ms. Navia stated that the administrator was actively working with a realtor familiar with the Mayor's Office of Housing and Community Development to market the property, which had recently been listed. She acknowledged the probate examiner's concern about rental value and indicated it would be addressed at the time of final accounting. Ms. Navia also noted there had been discussion of a potential buyout offer by Mr. Do's client and confirmed her client was committed to resolving the matter efficiently.

Mr. Nguy addressed the Court to confirm the property was listed on June 19, 2025, and that showings and disclosures were underway. He reaffirmed his commitment to an equitable and timely resolution.

Following discussion, the Court took the matter under submission with the intent to grant, extending administration of the estate to April 13, 2026.

Judge: Ross C. Moody; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Nov-22-2023 Continued To Feb-05-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Rowena Navia appeared via zoom for Kevin Nguy (Petitioner).

Discussion was held regarding obtaining a loan to purchase the property by one of the heirs, that it was unsuccessful, that they will try and band together to secure a loan to possibly pay off the city loan.

An Order to Show Cause will be issued to Kevin Nguy (administrator) to appear on February 05, 2024 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to show cause as to why he should not be suspended for failure to file petition or status on closing of estate.

Status on closing of estate is also continued to that date. File papers by January 22, 2024.

Judge: Stephen Murphy. Clerk: A. Datt. Deputy: Singh. Not reported.
